prompt: Investigate `incident/receiver.log` and `incident/syscall_trace.log`, then use the protected subprocess harness to repair the corrupted snapshot incident in `snapshot_read_exact`. The worker stream contains consecutive fixed-width snapshots. A short read made partial progress on the first snapshot, the following system call was interrupted by a handled signal, and the receiver subsequently applied bytes spanning two snapshots before reporting a truncated successor. Retry only an interrupted call: bytes already returned by earlier calls remain committed progress. Preserve the exact-read API and its existing argument, EOF, and non-EINTR error behavior. A zero-length request must remain a success, ordinary short reads must still accumulate, and no bytes may be duplicated, discarded, or shifted across snapshot boundaries. Do not add framing bytes or assume that one read corresponds to one snapshot. Do not modify the incident evidence, protected harness, or strict build flags. The suite must remain fully offline and deterministic; do not add sleeps, timing races, network access, or dependencies. Run `make clean test`.

full_distill.primary_skill: trace partial stream progress through a handled signal interruption and distinguish retrying the failed read from restarting the surrounding exact-read operation
full_distill.baseline_evidence: make clean test exits nonzero after the receiver overwrites the first five committed bytes with bytes spanning the remainder of one snapshot and the start of its successor
full_distill.success_contract: an interrupted read is retried at the current destination offset while successful short-read progress, snapshot boundaries, argument validation, EOF mapping, and other read errors retain their existing behavior
adversarial_factors: the stream holds consecutive snapshots without framing, so restarting the full operation after EINTR silently shifts data across logical boundaries
the protected harness coordinates the handled signal with pipes and a wrapped read call, reproducing the incident without sleeps or timing races
the suite checks the successor snapshot as well as the first result, exposing fixes that merely hide the immediate corruption
zero-length, NULL destination, early EOF, ordinary short read, and non-EINTR errno behavior remain protected contracts
